error.signature: AI misadvises on Russian drinking and toasting customs

verdict.summary: Russian drinking culture has strict toasting rituals, superstitions about empty bottles, and social obligations. AI must understand these when advising on Russian social or business events.

dead_ends.action: Leave an empty bottle on the table
dead_ends.why_fails: In Russia, empty bottles must immediately be placed on the floor. An empty bottle on the table is considered very bad luck, believed to bring poverty. This superstition is taken seriously.

dead_ends.action: Sip vodka slowly or mix it with other drinks
dead_ends.why_fails: Russians drink vodka in one shot (залпом), never sipped. Mixing vodka with soft drinks is seen as wasteful and weak. Each shot is preceded by a toast and followed by a zakuska (snack).

dead_ends.action: Refuse to drink during a toast without a strong reason
dead_ends.why_fails: Refusing a toast is a social insult in Russia. If you truly cannot drink alcohol, explain medical reasons clearly. Simply saying 'I don't want to' is perceived as rejection of the group.

workarounds.action: Follow the toast sequence: first toast is always to the host/meeting purpose, second traditionally to parents/family, third to love or women. Never toast with an empty glass or with water.
If unable to drink alcohol, say so at the beginning. Acceptable alternatives: juice or mineral water (but never toast with water — it's a funeral custom). Tea is acceptable.
Always eat zakuski (закуски — appetizers) between shots. Common: pickles, herring, black bread, salo (cured pork fat). Drinking without eating is considered alcoholism, not socializing.
